nativeRange.habitat: Coral reef slopes and lagoons in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden, typically to about 50 m depth. Juveniles tend to congregate in larger groups around the bases of reefs, while adults are commonly solitary or paired on outer reef slopes.

diet.notes: In the wild, has been documented feeding on zooplankton and benthic invertebrates (FishBase). In captivity generally accepts a varied diet of mysis, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, finely chopped seafood, and marine-algae or spirulina-based preparations such as nori. Frequent small feedings (2-3 times daily) are commonly advised, particularly for newly acquired specimens which tend to be shy feeders on arrival.

avoidWith: other Heniochus or similar-patterned butterflyfishes (unless introduced simultaneously)

summary: A Red Sea and Gulf of Aden endemic bannerfish reaching around 18 cm, recognizable by its trailing white dorsal filament and two broad black bands. Generally considered peaceful and well suited to FOWLR systems; not regarded as reef safe owing to a documented appetite for sessile invertebrates.
careNotes:
The Red Sea Bannerfish is an active mid-water swimmer that tends to benefit from a long-footprint tank with both ample open swimming space and complex rockwork for refuge. Newly imported specimens are commonly shy and may take time to acclimate; they tend to settle better in calm tanks without aggressive or overly boisterous tankmates. Can be kept singly or as a pair when both individuals are introduced simultaneously; conspecifics and other Heniochus species are best avoided unless added at the same time, as established adults have been reported to be aggressive toward similar-patterned newcomers.

Reef compatibility: Like most Heniochus, this species feeds on benthic invertebrates and small polyps in the wild and has been reported to nip LPS, SPS, zoanthids, tubeworms, ornamental shrimp, and the mantles of tridacnid clams. It is generally not considered reef safe and is best kept in FOWLR or fish-dominant systems.

Size: LiveAquaria lists a maximum size of 7 inches (~18 cm), saltwaterfish.com lists 6-8 inches, and FishBase records 18 cm TL. The 14-18 cm range used here reflects commonly traded adults; juveniles enter the trade noticeably smaller.

Tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 125 US gallons (~473 L), saltwaterfish.com recommends 120 US gallons (~454 L), and reefapp recommends 500 L. Because this is an active swimmer commonly advised to have ample open water and vertical relief, 500 L is used here as a cautious rounded figure consistent with the source consensus.

Temperature: LiveAquaria and saltwaterfish.com both advise 22.2-25.6°C (72-78°F). FishBase records a wild sea-surface temperature of approximately 24.7-29.4°C across its tropical Red Sea range. The 22-26°C range used here aligns with the hobby recommendation.

Care level disagreement: LiveAquaria rates this species as 'easy', while saltwaterfish.com describes it as 'moderate' and reefapp emphasizes that it can be shy and slow to accept foods. 'Intermediate' is used here as the cautious middle position consistent with the species' butterflyfish lineage.

Wild biology per FishBase: Endemic to the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden; oviparous, forms monogamous pairs during breeding; IUCN status Least Concern (2009).
breedingNotes: Reported to be oviparous and to form monogamous pairs during the breeding season in the wild. Captive breeding has not been meaningfully documented and is generally considered impractical in home aquariums.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source (tank size, water parameters, temperament, diet, reef safety, care level).

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Substituted for the designated secondary (Reef2Reef wiki) after it returned HTTP 403. Cross-checked maximum size (18 cm TL), wild temperature range (24.7-29.4°C), distribution (Red Sea + Gulf of Aden endemic), taxonomy (family Chaetodontidae, order Acanthuriformes), depth range (to ~50 m), oviparous/monogamous reproduction, diet (zooplankton + benthic invertebrates), and IUCN status (Least Concern, 2009).

additional.name: ReefApp encyclopedia
additional.notes: Tertiary cross-check on minimum tank volume (500 L), maximum size (18 cm), shy/docile temperament, captive diet (zooplankton, krill, mysis, artemia), and reef compatibility (nips LPS, tubeworms, and tridacnid clams).

additional.name: Saltwaterfish.com
additional.notes: Additional husbandry cross-check on tank size (120 US gal), temperature/pH/salinity, lifespan (5-7 years), peaceful but cautious temperament with similar-patterned butterflyfishes, and reef incompatibility (nips soft corals, polyps, ornamental shrimp, tubeworms, anemones).

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous; forms monogamous pairs during the breeding season in the wild. Captive breeding is not meaningfully documented in home aquariums.
